data.title: SM Energy announces post-merger leadership and board overhaul
data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

SM Energy ( (SM) ) has shared an update.

On November 2, 2025, SM Energy Company agreed to merge with Civitas Resources through a two-step transaction under which Civitas will first become a wholly owned subsidiary and then be merged into SM Energy, prompting a significant reshaping of the company’s board and leadership in January 2026 to align governance with the combined entity. On January 20, 2026, four directors submitted resignations effective at the closing of the first merger, the board was expanded to 11 members and six new directors, including industry veterans, were appointed along with extensive committee reassignments and dissolution of the Executive Committee; concurrently, effective at the closing of the second merger, Elizabeth A. McDonald was named president and chief executive officer and Blake D. McKenna executive vice president and chief operating officer, with detailed compensation packages, signaling a planned leadership transition and governance overhaul intended to guide the enlarged company post-merger while affirming that the changes do not stem from internal disagreements and involve no related-party conflicts.

The most recent analyst rating on (SM) stock is a Hold with a $23.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on SM Energy stock, see the SM Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on SM Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, SM is a Outperform.

The score is driven primarily by solid underlying financial performance (strong profitability and manageable leverage) and very attractive valuation (low P/E with a high dividend yield). These positives are tempered by weak technicals (below key moving averages with negative MACD) and elevated cash-flow risk from negative recent free cash flow, while corporate events are supportive given positive merger progress and targeted synergies.

To see Spark’s full report on SM stock, click here.

More about SM Energy

SM Energy Company is an independent oil and gas producer focused on exploration and development activities in U.S. basins, including the Permian and South Texas, with operations centered on unconventional resource plays and related strategic planning, field development and marketing within the energy sector.

Average Trading Volume: 3,151,720

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: $2.14B

Grayscale Stellar Lumens Trust ( (GXLM) ) just unveiled an update.

On January 20, 2026, CoinDesk Indices, the index provider for Grayscale Stellar Lumens Trust, altered the mix of digital asset trading platforms used to calculate the Trust’s XLM benchmark rate, adding Binance’s XLM-USDC pair while removing Bitfinex’s XLM-USD pair following its monthly review of venue eligibility. As of that date, the Index Price for the Trust is now based on XLM-USD trading pairs from Bitstamp by Robinhood, Crypto.com, Kraken, and OKX, and XLM-USDC trading pairs from Binance and Bybit, underscoring the ongoing role of exchange selection and periodic reviews in shaping the benchmark that underpins valuation and pricing for Trust stakeholders.

More about Grayscale Stellar Lumens Trust

Grayscale Stellar Lumens Trust (XLM) is a digital asset investment vehicle that offers exposure to Stellar Lumens (XLM), tracking a benchmark index price derived from trading activity across selected cryptocurrency exchanges. The Trust operates within the broader digital asset and crypto index industry, relying on third-party index calculation from CoinDesk Indices to determine its reference price for XLM via specified U.S. dollar and stablecoin trading pairs on vetted trading platforms.

data.title: Capital Bancorp Reports Record Q4 2025 Earnings Growth
data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Capital Bancorp ( (CBNK) ) has issued an announcement.

On January 26, 2026, Capital Bancorp reported fourth-quarter 2025 GAAP net income of $15.0 million, or $0.91 per diluted share, essentially flat versus the prior quarter but up sharply from $7.5 million a year earlier, as record full-year 2025 earnings were driven by double-digit balance sheet growth and a robust 1.71% return on assets. For the quarter ended December 31, 2025, the bank posted strong loan growth of 19.3% on an annualized basis and 12.5% year over year, deposit growth of 24.6% annualized from the third quarter and 12.0% from the prior year, expanding tangible book value per share by 3.4% from the third quarter and 15.5% from fourth-quarter 2024, while maintaining an allowance for credit losses of 1.85% of total loans and generating a net interest margin of 5.94% despite lower purchase accounting accretion. Core profitability metrics improved, with core ROA at 1.71%, core ROE at 15.23% and core ROTCE at 17.23%, and the company underscored its capital return and balance-sheet management by declaring a $0.12 per share cash dividend payable February 28, 2026 and repurchasing 304,288 shares in the quarter at an average price of $28.12, signaling continued confidence in its strategy and positioning for profitable organic growth and future bolt-on acquisitions following completion of the IFH integration.

The most recent analyst rating on (CBNK) stock is a Buy with a $34.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Capital Bancorp stock, see the CBNK Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on CBNK Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, CBNK is a Outperform.

The score is driven primarily by strong underlying financial performance (profitability, low leverage, and improved free cash flow) and supportive valuation (low P/E). These positives are tempered by a weaker technical profile, with the stock trading below key moving averages and subdued momentum. Recent corporate events are broadly constructive and add modest support.

To see Spark’s full report on CBNK stock, click here.

More about Capital Bancorp

Capital Bancorp, Inc., headquartered in Rockville, Maryland, is the holding company for Capital Bank, N.A., a commercial bank that generates revenue primarily through net interest income and fee-based services. The company focuses on diversified banking operations, including commercial lending and deposit gathering, and has pursued growth through both organic expansion and acquisitions, such as the recently integrated IFH transaction, while also investing in technology, human capital and opportunistic share repurchases to support its organic and acquisition-driven growth strategy.

data.title: Maris-Tech Tightens Note Conversion Ownership Cap and Scraps Planned Shareholder Meeting
data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Maris-Tech Ltd. ( (MTEK) ) has shared an announcement.

On January 26, 2026, Maris-Tech Ltd. amended the terms of $2 million in convertible promissory notes issued on November 25, 2025 to two institutional investors, cutting the beneficial ownership cap on conversions from 9.99% to 4.99% of its outstanding ordinary shares. To enforce this tighter ownership limit while still honoring conversion rights, any portion of a conversion (including future mandatory conversions at the 24‑month maturity mark) that would push an investor above the 4.99% threshold will now be settled in newly created pre-funded warrants rather than additional ordinary shares, with those warrants also subject to the same ownership cap; all other key economic terms of the notes and related purchase agreements remain unchanged, signaling a move to manage potential concentration of ownership and dilution. In a separate update, the company said it will not proceed with a previously announced shareholder meeting disclosed in December 2025 and indicated it will provide a further update if and when a new meeting is scheduled, leaving investors awaiting clarity on the timing and forum for future shareholder decisions.

The most recent analyst rating on (MTEK) stock is a Hold with a $1.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Maris-Tech Ltd. stock, see the MTEK Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on MTEK Stock

Maris-Tech Ltd.’s stock score is primarily impacted by its financial performance challenges, including profitability and liquidity issues. Technical analysis suggests potential bearish momentum, while valuation metrics highlight unprofitability concerns. The absence of earnings call data and corporate events limits additional insights.

To see Spark’s full report on MTEK stock, click here.

More about Maris-Tech Ltd.

The company’s shareholder base includes institutional holders subject to U.S. securities regulations and beneficial ownership limits under Section 13(d) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934.

data.title: Swedish Export Credit Corporation Posts Lower 2025 Profit but Delivers Record New Business and Strong Capital
data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The latest update is out from ELEMENTS Linked to the ICE BofAML Commodity Index eXtra Biofuels Total Return ( (FUE) ).

On January 26, 2026, Swedish Export Credit Corporation reported its year-end 2025 results, showing lower profitability but strong business volumes and a solid capital position. For January–December 2025, net interest income fell to Skr 2,719 million from Skr 3,058 million a year earlier, operating profit declined to Skr 1,491 million from Skr 2,121 million, and net profit dropped to Skr 1,183 million from Skr 1,683 million, pressured by lower Swedish short-term interest rates, a stronger krona and higher provisions for expected credit losses tied to a few exposures in the fourth quarter. The lending portfolio shrank 7.3% to Skr 262.7 billion, although sustainability-classified lending increased to Skr 56.3 billion, and the total capital ratio improved to 23.1% while total assets eased to Skr 350.0 billion. Despite weaker earnings and a proposed dividend of Skr 473 million, SEK recorded one of the highest volumes of new credit and guarantee commitments in its history at Skr 125.8 billion for 2025, including Skr 28.5 billion in the fourth quarter, supported by deals in European energy infrastructure, offshore wind, and fossil-free steel, as well as financing agreements with new corporate clients. Long-term borrowing surged to Skr 94 billion from Skr 53 billion in 2024, broadening the investor base and maintaining outstanding senior debt at Skr 304.6 billion, while the company’s strengthened credit facility with the Swedish National Debt Office and a new memorandum of understanding with Saudi Export-Import Bank further bolster its capacity to back Swedish exporters amid elevated geopolitical tensions and a gradually improving economic outlook.

More about ELEMENTS Linked to the ICE BofAML Commodity Index eXtra Biofuels Total Return

Aktiebolaget Svensk Exportkredit (AB SEK), or Swedish Export Credit Corporation, is a state-owned financial institution that provides export credits, guarantees and long-term financing to support the competitiveness of Swedish export companies. The company focuses on lending to large industrial, infrastructure and energy projects, including sustainability-classified and green financing, and funds its activities through active participation in international capital markets with long-term senior debt issuance in multiple currencies.

Learn more about FUE stock on TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.title: Vale Says Minas Gerais Water Overflows Contained, Dams Unaffected
data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Vale SA ( (VALE) ) just unveiled an announcement.

On January 26, 2026, Vale S.A. reported that water overflows detected on January 25 in the municipalities of Congonhas and Ouro Preto, in the Brazilian state of Minas Gerais, had been contained without injuries or impacts to nearby populations and communities. The company stressed that the incidents were not related to any of its dams in the region, that no mining tailings were released—only water with soil sediments—and that the stability and safety conditions of its dams remain unchanged and are monitored continuously. Vale said it routinely carries out preventive inspection and maintenance on its structures, with additional reinforcement during heavy rains, and that it is investigating the causes of the overflows so that any lessons learned can be incorporated into its rainfall management plans, while remaining available to authorities for further clarification, a stance aimed at reassuring stakeholders about its operational and environmental risk controls in a sensitive mining area.

The most recent analyst rating on (VALE) stock is a Hold with a $16.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Vale SA stock, see the VALE Stock Forecast page.

data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Ferrari ( (IT:RACE) ) has shared an update.

On January 26, 2026, Ferrari reported the latest progress in its €250 million share buyback program, which forms the first tranche of a planned €3.5 billion multi-year repurchase initiative to be executed by 2030. Between January 19 and January 23, 2026, the company repurchased 54,200 common shares on Euronext Milan for a total consideration of about €15.68 million, at an average price of €289.28 per share, bringing total purchases since the start of the multi-year program on January 5, 2026 to 138,700 shares for €42.26 million across Euronext Milan and the NYSE. As of January 23, 2026, Ferrari held 16,783,306 common shares in treasury, representing 8.65% of its issued common shares (9.12% including special voting shares), underscoring a significant ongoing capital return effort that tightens the free float and may support earnings per share and shareholder value over time.

More about Ferrari

Ferrari N.V. is a luxury automotive manufacturer headquartered in Maranello, Italy, best known for producing high-performance sports cars and grand tourers. Listed on both the New York Stock Exchange and Euronext Milan under the ticker RACE, the company targets the global premium and ultra-luxury car market and actively manages its capital structure through share buyback programs and equity incentive plans.

Maris-Tech Ltd. ( (MTEK) ) has provided an announcement.

On January 26, 2026, Maris-Tech Ltd. announced that it has reached a key integration milestone with U.S.-based space and defense technology firm Sidus Space for the LizzieSat-4 mission, as Maris-Tech’s advanced edge computing and video processing payload moves from planning into active hardware testing and integration. The payload, slated to fly aboard the LizzieSat-4 satellite later this year, will be tested and then mounted on Sidus’s modular, multi-mission platform to validate Maris-Tech’s high-performance edge computing and video processing capabilities in orbit, supporting real-time data handling and analytics for space and defense use cases. data.title: SMX Partners with TruCotton to Launch Molecular Traceability Initiative for U.S. Cotton
data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

SMX Public Limited Company ( (SMX) ) has provided an update.

On 26 January 2026, SMX (Security Matters) PLC announced a new initiative with TruCotton, a U.S. cotton traceability and branding program produced by WildWood Cotton Technologies, to develop full-chain verification of U.S.-grown cotton by embedding SMX’s molecular markers directly into the fiber. The collaboration aims to create a scientific, material-level method to prove cotton’s origin and authenticity throughout domestic and international supply chains, shifting traceability from documentation-based systems to tamper-resistant, machine-readable evidence that links physical cotton to secure digital records. By extending SMX’s proven textile-marking platform to U.S. cotton, the project is intended to enable a new class of premium, authenticated cotton inputs, strengthen brand and supply-chain integrity, and better support trade, compliance and consumer confidence in products marketed as U.S.-origin cotton, potentially enhancing the competitive positioning and value of U.S. cotton in global markets.

More about SMX Public Limited Company

SMX (Security Matters) PLC is a global provider of material-embedded molecular identity and digital traceability technology, offering marking, tracking, measuring and data platforms that help value-chain participants authenticate materials, support regulatory compliance and transition toward a lower-carbon, more transparent economy. Its solutions are applied across sectors including textiles, recycled materials and industrial supply chains, where persistent, physical-level markers are used to verify origin, quality and integrity of products as they move through complex global networks.
